Title: Enhancing malting performance of harder barley varieties through ultrasound treatment

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ABA biosynthetic cleavage step from carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, a key precursor of abscisic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains seed dormancy and inhibits germination
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during early germination/8 h after germination
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript level in Ganpi-6 and Kenpi-14 than Copeland at 8 h after germination; lower in Ganpi-6 after ultrasound treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed imbibition/germination; ultrasound treatment during malting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher NCED1 expression is associated with poorer germination-related malting performance and lower water uptake in harder barley; ultrasound reduces its expression in Ganpi-6
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR comparison during germination and after ultrasound treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCED1 was identified as a major ABA biosynthesis gene monitored during barley germination. 2. Its expression was higher in the harder domestic barley varieties Ganpi-6 and Kenpi-14 than in Copeland at early germination. 3. Ultrasound treatment reduced NCED1 transcript abundance in Ganpi-6, consistent with improved malting traits.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher expression is associated with delayed germination and poorer malting quality
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lowering NCED1 expression/activity may help improve germination and malting performance of hard-kernel barley; useful target for selection or treatment-responsive screening
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper uses expression analysis only; ABA content itself was not directly quantified

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ABA biosynthetic cleavage step from carotenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, a key precursor of abscisic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains seed dormancy and inhibits germination
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during early germination/8 h after germination
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript level in Ganpi-6 and Kenpi-14 than Copeland at 8 h after germination; lower in Ganpi-6 after ultrasound treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed imbibition/germination; ultrasound treatment during malting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher NCED2 expression is associated with poorer germination-related malting performance in harder barley; ultrasound reduces its expression in Ganpi-6
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR comparison during germination and after ultrasound treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCED2 was monitored as a major ABA biosynthesis gene during barley germination. 2. It showed higher expression in hard domestic barley relative to Copeland. 3. Ultrasound treatment reduced NCED2 expression in Ganpi-6 in parallel with improved malting quality.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher expression is associated with reduced germination vigor and poorer malting quality
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving malting quality by reducing ABA biosynthesis during germination
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is correlative from transcript profiling; no direct ABA measurement or mutant validation

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursors of bioactive gibberellins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes successive oxidation steps in late gibberellin biosynthesis to generate precursors of bioactive GAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes seed germination and induction of hydrolytic enzymes during malting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in Ganpi-6 and Kenpi-14 than Copeland at 8 h after germination; higher in Ganpi-6 after ultrasound treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed germination; ultrasound treatment during steeping/malting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher GA20ox1 expression is associated with better germination-related malting quality, increased hydrolytic enzyme activities, and improved water uptake response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR comparison across cultivars and after ultrasound treatment; association with malting enzyme activities and quality traits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA20ox1 was analyzed as a key GA biosynthesis gene during germination. 2. Its expression was lower in hard domestic barley than in Copeland. 3. Ultrasound treatment increased GA20ox1 expression in Ganpi-6, coinciding with improved malting performance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced expression is associated with poor malting quality; no direct agronomic penalty tested for increased expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker or target for breeding barley with stronger germination and malting quality, especially in hard-kernel varieties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper infers GA pathway activation from transcripts; GA levels were not measured directly

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: modifies GA pool/homeostasis associated with germination performance
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 2-oxidation of gibberellins or their precursors, affecting GA metabolic balance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates seed germination and hydrolytic enzyme induction through GA homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reported among GA synthesis genes as lower in Ganpi-6 and Kenpi-14 than Copeland; increased by ultrasound in Ganpi-6 at end of germination according to text
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed germination; ultrasound treatment during malting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression changes were associated with improved GA-related germination behavior and malting quality after ultrasound treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR comparison across cultivars and ultrasound treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA2ox3 was included by the authors among GA synthesis/metabolism genes linked to germination. 2. Its expression was lower in harder domestic barley than in Copeland. 3. Ultrasound treatment increased GA-pathway gene expression in Ganpi-6 along with better malting quality.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker of GA metabolic status during malting, though mechanistic role in this study remains less resolved than GA20ox1/GA3ox2
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although GA2ox genes are classically GA deactivation enzymes, this paper grouped GA2ox3 with GA synthesis genes; extraction follows the paper’s presentation while noting this caveat

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 3β-hydroxylation of GA precursors to form bioactive gibberellins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes germination and hydrolytic enzyme production important for malting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in Ganpi-6 and Kenpi-14 than Copeland at early germination; higher in ultrasound-treated Ganpi-6 by end of germination
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed germination; ultrasound treatment during malting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher GA3ox2 expression is associated with improved germination and better malting quality in barley; ultrasound enhanced its expression in Ganpi-6
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR comparison across cultivars and after ultrasound treatment; association with enzyme activities and malt quality measurements
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA3ox2 was analyzed as a key GA biosynthetic gene during barley germination. 2. Its expression was lower in hard domestic barley than in Copeland. 3. Ultrasound treatment increased GA3ox2 expression in Ganpi-6, paralleling improved malting-related traits.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target or marker for selecting barley lines with stronger germination and improved malting quality
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GA contents were not directly quantified; conclusions are based on transcript and phenotype associations
